quizQuestion:
Lo hizo {___} amor, no {___} dinero.
(He did it for love, not for money.)
[Both blanks: same preposition]
sentenceTranslation: He did it for love, not for money.
explanation: por — expressing cause or motive. Por amor = for (the cause of) love. Por dinero = for (the motive of) money. Key rule: por expresses the reason/cause behind an action (looking backward at the cause).

quizQuestion:
El libro fue escrito {___} un autor famoso.
(The book was written by a famous author.)
sentenceTranslation: The book was written by a famous author.
explanation: por — the agent in a passive construction is introduced by por. El libro fue escrito por... = The book was written by... This is distinct from para which would indicate for (recipient/purpose).

quizQuestion:
Lo cambié {___} otro modelo más nuevo.
(I exchanged it for a newer model.)
sentenceTranslation: I exchanged it for a newer model.
explanation: por — expressing exchange or trade. Cambiar X por Y = to exchange X for Y. Lo cambié por otro = I exchanged it for another. Also: lo vendí por 50 euros = I sold it for 50 euros.

quizQuestion:
Estudié {___} tres horas sin parar.
(I studied for three hours without stopping.)
sentenceTranslation: I studied for three hours without stopping.
explanation: por — expressing duration (how long something lasted). Estudié por tres horas = I studied for three hours. Contrast with para which expresses a deadline: lo necesito para el lunes = I need it by Monday.

quizQuestion:
Pasamos {___} el centro de la ciudad.
(We passed through the city center.)
sentenceTranslation: We passed through the city center.
explanation: por — expressing movement through or along a place. Pasar por = to pass through. Caminar por la playa = to walk along the beach. Contrast: para Madrid = headed to/toward Madrid (destination).

quizQuestion:
Gano veinte euros {___} hora.
(I earn twenty euros per hour.)
sentenceTranslation: I earn twenty euros per hour.
grammarPointLabel: por: rate/per (euros por hora, tres veces por semana)
explanation: por — expressing rate or frequency. Veinte euros por hora = twenty euros per hour. Tres veces por semana = three times per week. This is the 'per' sense of por: a fixed quantity per unit.

quizQuestion:
Firmé el documento {___} él, porque estaba enfermo.
(I signed the document on his behalf, because he was sick.)
sentenceTranslation: I signed the document on his behalf, because he was sick.
grammarPointLabel: por: on behalf of / in place of
explanation: por — expressing acting on behalf of or in place of someone. Firmé por él = I signed for/instead of him. Habló por todos = she spoke for everyone. This distinguishes from para (purpose/recipient): firmé para él = I signed it for him (as a gift).

quizQuestion:
Te llamé tres veces {___} teléfono.
(I called you three times by phone.)
sentenceTranslation: I called you three times by phone.
grammarPointLabel: por: communication medium (por teléfono, por correo)
explanation: por — expressing the medium or means of communication. Por teléfono = by phone. Por correo = by mail. Por internet = via the internet. Por email = by email. This is a fixed expression pattern.

quizQuestion:
Estudio {___} aprender, no {___} aprobar.
(I study to learn, not to pass.)
[Both blanks: same preposition]
sentenceTranslation: I study to learn, not to pass.
explanation: para — expressing purpose or goal. Para + infinitive = in order to. Estudio para aprender = I study in order to learn. This is the most basic para usage: the purpose of an action.

quizQuestion:
Esta carta es {___} María.
(This letter is for María.)
explanation: para — expressing the recipient (who something is intended for). Esta carta es para María = This letter is for María (she will receive it). Compare por: Esta carta fue escrita por María (por = agent who wrote it).

quizQuestion:
Salgo {___} Madrid mañana por la mañana.
(I'm leaving for Madrid tomorrow morning.)
sentenceTranslation: I'm leaving for Madrid tomorrow morning.
explanation: para — expressing destination when there is intentional movement toward a goal. Salgo para Madrid = I'm leaving for/toward Madrid. Compare por: pasé por Madrid = I passed through Madrid.

quizQuestion:
Lo necesito {___} el lunes a primera hora.
(I need it by Monday first thing.)
sentenceTranslation: I need it by Monday first thing.
grammarPointLabel: para: deadline (para el lunes = by Monday)
explanation: para — expressing a deadline (by/by the time of). Para el lunes = by Monday. This is a key para vs. por distinction: para lunes (deadline) vs. por lunes (approximate time: around Monday).

quizQuestion:
{___}, es demasiado difícil este problema.
(For me / In my opinion, this problem is too difficult.)
sentenceTranslation: For me, this problem is too difficult.
explanation: Para mí — 'for me / in my opinion'. Para + pronoun expresses opinion or viewpoint. Para mí, para ti, para él... Compare with: a mí me parece (expressing how something seems to you).

quizQuestion:
{___} principiante, habla muy bien el español.
(For a beginner, she speaks Spanish very well.)
[Hint: unexpected contrast — para + ser + noun]
sentenceTranslation: For a beginner, she speaks Spanish very well.
grammarPointLabel: para: unexpected contrast (para ser... = for a/an...)
explanation: Para ser — expressing unexpected contrast. Para ser principiante = for a beginner (given that she is a beginner, her level is surprisingly good). This is a sophisticated para usage expressing contrast between expectation and reality.

quizQuestion:
Trabaja {___} una empresa internacional en Barcelona.
(She works for an international company in Barcelona.)
sentenceTranslation: She works for an international company in Barcelona.
grammarPointLabel: para: employment (trabajar para = work for an employer)
explanation: para — expressing employment relationship (who you work for). Trabaja para una empresa = she works for a company (she is employed by them). Compare por: trabajó por él = he worked in his place / on his behalf.
